scenario: Axum Handler trait propagation and request context layer assessment

map_quality.rationale: Lists 5 Handler impls with exact file:line (handler/mod.rs:208, 227, 271, 317; method_routing.rs:1355), plus the HandlerService adapter bridge at service.rs:146/165/171. Type-state markers (T, S) are spelled out per impl, and closure-invocation lines are pinpointed (e.g., :255, :278, :332-343) — the agent can jump straight to the edit site.

specificity.rationale: Cites the macro layer concretely: `impl_handler!` at :227 expanded by `all_the_tuples!` at :262, with the M discriminator marker explained. Trait bounds are reproduced verbatim (e.g., `impl<T, S> Handler<private::IntoResponseHandler, S> for T where T: IntoResponse`) rather than glossed.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY each impl exists: IntoResponseHandler for response-only types (req ignored, line 278), Layered for tower composition via `with_state` + `layer.oneshot`, MethodRouter delegation. The M marker rationale (`coherence-workaround marker carrying argument-shape`) addresses why the type-state exists.

uncertainty.rationale: Does not flag axum-extra's HandlerCallWithExtractors extension or explicitly note that the macro-generated impls (16 arity tuples) are not enumerated individually — the agent must trust `all_the_tuples!` covers them. No caveat about user/third-party Handler impls in bounds.

map_quality.rationale: Provides FromRequestParts at axum-core/src/extract/mod.rs:53, FromRequest at :79, FromRef at from_ref.rs:14, lists 20+ built-in extractors with file:line, and maps the rejection short-circuit path through handler/mod.rs:241-246. An agent could wire a new extractor with only tiny lookups.

specificity.rationale: Exact trait names with concrete bounds (`impl<Outer, Inner> FromRequestParts<Outer> for State<Inner> where Inner: FromRef<Outer>`), exact ViaParts/ViaRequest marker explanation, and per-extractor file:line citations rather than vague references.

justification.rationale: Explains the two-trait split via the `ViaParts` blanket and coherence marker, why State uses FromRef (substate from one Router<AppState>), and the rejection-via-IntoResponse contract. Could be slightly more explicit on body-consumption rationale but covers the WHY clearly.

uncertainty.rationale: Includes a 'writing a new extractor' guidance block flagging the FromRequestParts-vs-FromRequest choice and Infallible rejection. Doesn't explicitly flag user-defined extractors in axum-extra or subtler rejection-conversion pitfalls like tuple unification to Response.

map_quality.rationale: All 7 layers are walked with precise file:line citations (serve/mod.rs:103, :321, :559; routing/mod.rs:452/614; path_router.rs:325; method_routing.rs:1167/1355; handler/mod.rs:153/227/255; handler/service.rs:165). The agent could place middleware at the correct layer directly from this map.

specificity.rationale: Exact method names used throughout: `call_with_state`, `oneshot_inner_owned`, `serve_connection_with_upgrades`, `TowerToHyperService::new` (serve/mod.rs:596), `set_matched_path_for_request`, `BoxedIntoRoute`. No vague 'the call method' phrasing.

justification.rationale: Explains role of each layer — why PathRouter/MethodRouter are split (trie lookup vs method dispatch), why the Tower-to-hyper bridge exists, what IntoResponse does at the boundary (line 255 `.into_response()`), and why extensions survive the parts/body round-trip.

uncertainty.rationale: Notes the axum-core vs axum split implicitly via file paths and flags the Router::layer vs route_layer interaction with catch_all_fallback, but doesn't explicitly call out alternative serve paths (hyper directly vs axum::serve) the agent should verify.

steps.step: Assess adding a request context layer

map_quality.rationale: Concrete edit map with new file paths, an extract/mod.rs:30-36 re-export site, and user-side wiring; cites Router::layer at routing/mod.rs:296 and route_layer at :313 with the catch_all_fallback lines 304-308. An agent could open a PR directly from this.

specificity.rationale: Tradeoffs are grounded in actual surfaces — `Router::layer` vs `route_layer` with exact line numbers, MatchedPath set at path_router.rs:347, and the parts/body round-trip at lines 340/360 — not abstract middleware talk.

justification.rationale: Explicitly explains WHY the Extension+extractor approach is least-breaking (mirrors MatchedPath precedent, zero Handler/MethodRouter/HandlerService changes) and enumerates what Handler-trait modification would break across 5 impls and third-party bounds.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the layer-vs-route_layer 404 interaction and the Send+'static extensions constraint, and notes the parts/body round-trip preserves extensions. Doesn't explicitly call out nested router (`Router::nest`) or tower middleware ordering subtleties the agent should verify.
